text:
Once upon an autumn moonlit night.

There lived a wise old owl named Whistleslavitch.

He loved poetry. Every dusk.

With his beakful feathers.

Whisperslavitch loved words.

He danced.

Around his moonlit hearthruff.

With soft feathered feet.

Of tales bold.

Of dragons.

And spells.

Of love ballads.

Of rainbows.

Oh dear friends.

How I whistle.

Their secrets.

Of life!

So wise Owl Whistleslavitch.

You know.

They loved.

Just owl stories.



Those days.

I wouldn't miss.



Just birds.

I'd never know.

But never forget.




Just owl.

That cared.




Sometimes.


Just owl stories.




That mattered.



Okay owl owlets.

I fly.

You think.



Our forest loves whispers.




Just owl tales.




That rhyme.



Oh wow.



A little bird?

I'll tell.

Anything.











Whistleslavitch whistled.

"To find friends good."


Just owl loves.




Of owl owl
